item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: 1938.jpg
Author: Roget
Release year: 2012

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's like a tank that lets you teleport inside as a fellow SCP-1938-1 or 2? By "Positively interact" do you mean they talk? or do you just telepathically know what their emotions or thoughts are? What happens if you fill it with other stuff? like Kool-aid or acid?
And the most important question of all: DO THE FISH WEAR TINY CUTE HATS AND EQUIPMENT?! I NEED TO KNOW!!!!!!!!!!!!! AAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!

I have so many questions and this document fails to answer most of them.

Neutral. (unless you can explain that stuff.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: This user is permanently banned for vehemence and harassment.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
The timer has expired, and the edits have been applied in accordance with recieved feedback. The author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide proposed edits

This link was also suggested by the author, and the fact that 1938 is based around a huuman temporarily transported into the fish's world while SCP-1839 is a person convinced they're a fish lent to easy linkage. I placed the link where I did to put the link in iin the most unobtrusive manner possible.

Editing from this:

Additionally, if any adolescent person (hereafter referred to as "the user") submerges any portion of their body into SCP-1938, they will appear to vanish for approximately fifteen minutes, after which they will re-appear in a spot within 15 meters of their original location. Users will report that they were teleported into SCP-1938 in the form of a small fish, and that they were in a feudal-style underwater civilization populated by instances of SCP-1938-1. This civilization is usually described as having characteristics similar to the traditional interpretation of a "Utopia", with every instance of SCP-1938-1 reporting great satisfaction with their life and an eagerness to positively interact with the user. The time the user perceives having spent in SCP-1938 ranges from 2 hours to one week. Note that SCP-1938-1's behavior does not reflect the observations of the user during this time.

If any species of fish that are not instances of SCP-1938-1 are introduced into the tank, all instances of SCP-1938-1 will immediately change into copies of this introduced species. These new organisms (hereafter referred to as SCP-1938-2) will behave in a much more erratic manner than instances of SCP-1938-1, but apart from behavior and appearance, they share the properties of SCP-1938-1.

To this:

Additionally, if any adolescent person (hereafter referred to as "the user") submerges any portion of their body into SCP-1938, they will appear to vanish for approximately fifteen minutes, after which they will re-appear in a spot within 15 meters of their original location. Users will report that they were teleported into SCP-1938 in the form of a small fish, and that they were in a feudal-style underwater civilization populated by instances of SCP-1938-1. This civilization is usually described as having characteristics similar to the traditional interpretation of a "Utopia", with every instance of SCP-1938-1 reporting great satisfaction with their life and an eagerness to positively interact with the user. The time the user perceives having spent in SCP-1938 ranges from 2 hours to one week. Note that SCP-1938-1's behavior does not reflect the observations of the user during this time.

Of particular note are several interactions where instances of SCP-1938-1 have inquired after Foundation personnel under the effects of SCP-1839. It is unknown where or through what means the instances of SCP-1938-1 acquired their knowledge, and further investigation is recommended to ensure containment of both anomalies is properly maintained.

If any species of fish that are not instances of SCP-1938-1 are introduced into the tank, all instances of SCP-1938-1 will immediately change into copies of this introduced species. These new organisms (hereafter referred to as SCP-1938-2) will behave in a much more erratic manner than instances of SCP-1938-1, but apart from behavior and appearance, they share the properties of SCP-1938-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed reading this one, and I'm generally fan of wondertainment, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… such as a field that makes it anomalously light? 450 litres of water should have a mass of 450kg. If it is anomalously light, you should say so. As it is, it's just confusing and distracting.

Rest of it is good, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if SPC want this.

It would be very useful for multiple shark-punching practice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, you're cool. Necro-posting is acceptable when you have something new to add to the conversation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Also adding new fish doesn't make them act overaggressive at anyone using the tank, but rather they out-compete the fake species wondertainment invented and start messing up their culture."

They aren't a fake species, botia macracantha is the scientific name for the clown loach. They're a freshwater species that's social and rather clown like (one of mine is currently swimming laps around a bubble line like it's new or something). They do live in the wild, and in fish tanks, and make wonderful pets. So while I know SCPs aren't supposed to be things you'd want to own, I want this one.

I'd upvote twice if I could (and I'm sorry about posting on an old thread)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't make sense to me that you can fill a 25kg tank with 450 litres of water and have it weigh only 55kg. Remember, one litre of water weighs one kilogram. If you put 30 litres in (which would make it weigh 55kg), the tank would only be about 7% full.

Of course, the tank might just warp space or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very Wondertainment like! At first a normal thing, at second glance a dangerous, unpredictable, and most of all disturbing piece. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr. Wondertainment is not responsible for any harm sustained as a result of Explosions!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Explosions!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is the very least child friendly thing it could possibly do in that situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It will explode

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you empty the tank during the fifteen-minute window that someone's disappeared?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent! I would upvote, had I not already done so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a bit to the civilization bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that statement deserver a lot of elaboration. It also does a lot to hurt the tone…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not on board with the "medieval-style underwater civilization" either, but this is pretty great. Wondertainment felt tacked on until I thought back over the whole thing with that in mind; it gives there a reason for the whole civilization deal.

I think I'd love to see some interviews with a D-class who experiences both halves of the civilization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Starfish loves you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is other stuff that the tank generates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I figured it was time for another non-lethal wondertainment item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description: SCP-1938 is an aquarium, capable of holding up to 450 liters of water. It weighs approximately 25 kilograms when it is empty, and 55 kilograms when it has been filled. SCP-1938's area measures 151 x 61 cm, and is 63 cm in height.

ok, where did the other 420kg go?

and that's assuming it's JUST filled with water, with no (denser than water!) sand/rocks/furniture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somewheeeeere, under the seeeeaaaa an uuuuupvoooteee is waiting for theeeee…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feex for reals this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Use the dimensions of a 450 litre tank, it'll be easier for you to get details that aren't wonky when you're working off things that are commonly produced (though not for children.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Bugger. Let me just….change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And a tank capable of holding that much water is going to be REALLY heavy when empty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is cute. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description: SCP-1938 is an aquarium, capable of holding up to 1000 liters of water. It weighs approximately 15 kilograms when it is empty, and 55 kilograms when it has been filled. It has an area of approximately 1x2 meters, with a height of .5 meters.

just to be clear - you're intending this thing to be anomalously light? 1000L of water is literally one metric ton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a brilliant target audience.

"Well, we've been selling toys to small children for a decade now, it's getting stale, what do we do now?"
"Well…"
"Yes, Bob?"
"What if we… what if we sell toys to huge children?"
"What?"
"Like, 8 foot tall children with the muscles of gorillas?"
"…BRILLIANT!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking of which, who is Wondertainment trying to sell this too?

children.

…the size of Fernand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Adds a turtle.
Suddenlyteenagemutantninjaturtles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Dr. Wondertainment angle seems tacked on. I think you could take it out and rework the article slightly to make it more interesting. A weird race of sapient fish that someone can join? That's interesting enough without having to be Dr. Wondertainment.

I also don't like the effect when regular fish are added. Why would all of the weird fish change to match?

Withholding vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every user has a unique experience each time they go in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd go into more detail on the 1938-1 fish biology and culture, as well as making it so you could see odd stuff like the castles they made from the outside of the tank, and they had assorted species such as "seahorses™", "wheat-algae™" or "sea-dragons™" which are simply aquatic parodies of a stranded fantasy kingdom setting. Maybe even change 1938-1 from "fish" to "something like the fake sea monkeys shown in advertisements." Also adding new fish doesn't make them act overaggressive at anyone using the tank, but rather they out-compete the fake species wondertainment invented and start messing up their culture.

A few other, weirder ideas might be a guide to the 1938-1 culture, major kingdoms and religions, and/or changing the tank so its only property is the "you vanish and think you're a fish in it." 1938-1 is just a weird species of self-aware fish which lives in it and was apparently made by wondertainment, but can live in the wild just as well.

Even so I quite like this idea so, upvote.

P.S. what would happen if you introduced Lord Blackwood in his normal sea-slug form?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this. Upboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking of which, who is Wondertainment trying to sell this too? Tanks that large need more than a couple people to carry when empty, this thing is stocked with rock and gravel. Also what is the tank made of? Acrylic or glass?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeah, that bothered me, too.

Description: SCP-1938 is an aquarium, capable of holding up to 15 liters of water. It weighs approximately .5 kilograms when it is empty, and 15 kilograms when filled. It has an area of approximately 1x2 meters, with a height of .5 meters.

2x1x.5m is one cubic meter; that's a 250gal tank. a 250gal aquarium that only weighs .5kg empty? unless it's made of magical mystery materials (which would be trite and distracting), this should be more like 50-100kg - empty.

and the capacity? 15L of water in a 1000L space. obviously a lot of volume should be used by sand and rocks and such, but…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh I'll fix that asap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So much potential!

medieval-style underwater civilization <— This thing could be a research topic on its own. How do they communicate? Do they use any tools, farms? What do they eat? What do they do? etc.

Also, can multiple users go into the tank at the same time? (multiplayer?) Can they affect the civilization to what degree, is it revertable? (Maybe everything resets when water is emptied and refilled?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun fact, I got the idea for this article when I was cleaning a fish tank very similar to this one. I began wondering "What if I was a fish?…" and it went from there.

I am a very strange man…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a bit of that Southpark episode with the sea people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not to mention that the depicted tank looks to be a 120 gallon (about 450+ liters) and contains a Clownfish and a Moorish Idol.

*I misjudged the size of the fish, my mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You do realize a tank measuring 1 m x 2 m x 50 cm is not capable of holding fifteen liters of water? Try a thousand.

Anyway, apart from that, upvoted because I like it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
medieval-style underwater civilization

Hmmm, do you mean "feudal-style"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't feel this really comes across as Wondertainmenty, but I do quite like that it absolutely cannot injure you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. I like the alterations you made from the original draft, too. +1ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … So does Wondertainment have Merlin working on staff now? o.o Soooo upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahem. Even though I'm getting tired of Wondertainment SCPs, and this could totally work alone, I enjoyed it and really like the idea.

Upboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 For Dr.Wondertainment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …where can I get my own?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are a tuna head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chaaaarlieeeee

Chaaaarlieeeee

We're underwaaaaater, Chaaaarlieeeee!
